input.code_to_execute:
queries = [
    'site:developers.hubspot.com HubSpot private app access tokens official',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com HubSpot OAuth 2.0 official',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com HubSpot GraphQL official',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com HubSpot API rate limits official',
    'site:hubspot.com HubSpot API limits pricing official',
    'site:github.com hubspot mcp server official',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com model context protocol HubSpot official'
]
results = {}
for q in queries:
    r, e = web_search(q)
    results[q] = {'error': e, 'result': r[:4000] if isinstance(r, str) else str(r)[:4000]}
print(results)

input.code_to_execute:
queries = [
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "private apps" "Authorization: Bearer" HubSpot',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "OAuth" "authorization code" HubSpot',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "GraphQL API" HubSpot',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "API usage guidelines and limits" HubSpot',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "Model Context Protocol" HubSpot',
    'site:developers.hubspot.com "hs mcp setup" HubSpot'
]
for q in queries:
    r, e = web_search(q)
    print('\nQUERY:', q)
    print('ERROR:', e)
    print(r[:6000] if isinstance(r, str) else str(r)[:6000])

raw_result.description: CRM platform with APIs for customer data, marketing, sales, service, and CMS workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official HubSpot docs support OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for public apps and Bearer access tokens for private apps; the available evidence also indicates OAuth 2.1 with PKCE for HubSpot's remote MCP server. Private app documentation URL could not be directly opened in-browser due to login redirect, so token-auth details rely on gathered official-search evidence and should be human-verified.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer docs are available, HubSpot offers self-serve developer access, and API usage is available without partner approval; limits vary by subscription tier and add-ons.

api_surface.breadth: Broad API surface across CRM objects, marketing, sales, service, CMS, rate-limit/account endpoints, and an official GraphQL API in addition to REST APIs.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered indicates HubSpot provides official MCP offerings: a remote HubSpot MCP server at mcp.hubspot.com and a local developer MCP flow via HubSpot CLI (`hs mcp setup`).

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered official evidence indicates HubSpot enforces subscription- and app-type-based limits. Public OAuth apps are limited to 110 requests per 10 seconds. Private apps have burst and daily limits that vary by tier, with published API-limit pricing/add-on details and 429 responses on overage.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official GraphQL support is documented alongside REST APIs. HubSpot appears to have official MCP support, which is a strong positive signal for agent tooling. API-limit increases are productized on a pricing page rather than being partner-gated.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Some details, especially private-app token auth specifics and exact self-serve/free availability nuances, were inferred from gathered official-search evidence rather than directly opened source pages, and the private-app page encountered a login redirect during browser research.
